mission.title: Install and Configure Kanister on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kanister is an extensible framework for application-level data management on Kubernetes, designed to simplify data protection workflows. Installing Kanister allows you to efficiently manage backup, restore, and data operations across various storage solutions in a Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Kanister Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Kanister Helm repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kanister https://charts.kanister.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kanister using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kanister in your Kubernetes cluster with the following command, creating a namespace called 'kanister':
```bash
helm install kanister kanister/fdb-instance --namespace kanister --create-namespace --version 0.118.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kanister pods are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kanister
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Kanister deployment by editing the values.yaml file and applying it:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment kanister --namespace kanister
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Kanister
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to ensure Kanister has the necessary permissions:
```bash
kubectl create role kanister-role --verb=get,list,watch,create,update,delete --resource=pods --namespace kanister
kubectl create rolebinding kanister-rolebinding --role=kanister-role --serviceaccount=kanister:default --namespace kanister
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Kanister Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kanister release from your Kubernetes cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall kanister --namespace kanister
```

uninstall.title: Clean up Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Remove any CRDs that were created during the installation:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep kanister.io | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Kanister namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Kanister to clean up any remaining resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kanister
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configurations and data:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kanister -o yaml > kanister-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Kanister Helm chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kanister
upgrade.description:
Upgrade your Kanister installation to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade kanister kanister/fdb-instance --namespace kanister --version 0.118.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Kanister pods to ensure they are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kanister
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your Kanister pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kanister
# Look for events indicating resource issues
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating the Helm release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace kanister
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not available
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot find the Kanister CRDs, verify they were installed correctly:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kanister.io
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient permissions
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive permission errors, check your RBAC configuration:
```bash
kubectl get rolebinding --namespace kanister
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kanister will result in all pods running in the 'kanister' namespace, with no errors reported in the pod logs. You should also be able to access the Kanister APIs for managing data operations.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kanister https://charts.kanister.io
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment kanister --namespace kanister
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources

kubectl create role kanister-role --verb=get,list,watch,create,update,delete --resource=pods --namespace kanister
kubectl create rolebinding kanister-rolebinding --role=kanister-role --serviceaccount=kanister:default --namespace kanister

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
